# Tool to extract all dependencies.

This is an internal tool to display all the dependencies in a nice looking graph. This can be used to analyze which dependencies are causing you issues, or why a certain library is needed by your executable.

Note: Parsing the .dot file can take a while, as these files are pretty big, so just be patient.

## Usage

* First you must install the tool by running `. ./configure.sh`. This will create a virtual environment with the tool
* Next you must generate a cmake file with a graphviz dot file, this can be done my passing the `--graphviz=<mydot.dot>` flag to cmake.

For example on linux:

```
$HOME/src/android/emu-master-dev/prebuilts/cmake/linux-x86/bin/cmake -B$HOME/src/android/emu-master-dev/out -G Ninja -DOPTION_CRASHUPLOAD=PROD -DBREAKPAD_API_URL=https://prod-crashsymbolcollector-pa.googleapis.com -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=$HOME/src/android/emu-master-dev/external/qemu/android/build/cmake/toolchain-linux-x86_64.cmake -DPython_EXECUTABLE=/usr/bin/python3 -DQTWEBENGINE=True -DGFXSTREAM=True $HOME/src/android/emu-master-dev/external/qemu --graphviz=$HOME/src/android/emu-master-dev/out/mydot.dot
```

Note: Do not copy paste this command as is, obtain it from running `android/rebuild.sh` make sure to add the flag ` --graphviz=$HOME/src/android/emu-master-dev/out/mydot.dot`


Once you have generated this `mydot.dot` file you can analyze it with this script. For example:

```
$ emu-deps $HOME/src/android/emu-master-dev/out/mydot.dot --target android-emu_unittests
```
